html:
The Backpack is a Survival Item that serves to expand the character's inventory. It requires 4 Cut Grass and 4 Twigs to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. The Backpack itself cannot be placed in the inventory or in Chests and only one may be equipped at a time.

When a Backpack is created, it automatically equips itself in the Chest Slot if it is available. Wearing the Backpack provides 8 additional inventory slots. This item uses the same equipment slot as Armor and Vests, which means wearing one requires sacrificing the benefits of the other. Unlike other items that go into the Chest slot, Backpacks do not have durability.

Although Backpacks cannot be placed in the inventory or in Chests, they can be dropped on the ground and used for storage. Any item hovered over a Backpack while it's on the ground will display the 'Store' option, allowing the pack to be filled without equipping it.

Downloadable Content

In all DLCs, the Backpack is a flammable item. When set on fire, everything inside won't be affected by fire but the Backpack will eventually burn to Ashes, dropping anything it had inside. Care should be taken when dropping a backpack near a campfire to prevent it from burning. The backpack is not waterproof, and can thus get soaked and drain the player's Sanity, but items in the backpack will stay completely dry. If the player does not have full water protection, it is recommended to keep one's fuel and food items in a backpack during the rain.

In the Hamlet DLC, Backpacks can be bought in the Pigg and Pigglet's General Store for 5 Oincs.

Trivia

  • The Backpack was added in the The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You update.

Bugs

  • A backpack can be burned and extinguished to be able to keep it in one's inventory. Relogging will reset this.
  • Sometimes the player can't pick up a backpack in the Caves if it is dropped due to the player's death.

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Equipped

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Wilson wearing a Backpack.
  • Willow wearing a Backpack.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Backpack.
  • Wendy wearing a Backpack.
  • WX-78 wearing a Backpack.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Backpack.
  • Woodie wearing a Backpack.
  • Wes wearing a Backpack.
  • Maxwell wearing a Backpack.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Backpack.
  • Webber wearing a Backpack.
  • Walani wearing a Backpack.
  • Warly wearing a Backpack.
  • Wilbur wearing a Backpack.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Backpack.
  • Wilba wearing a Backpack.
  • Wormwood wearing a Backpack.
  • Wheeler wearing a Backpack.
  • Winona wearing a Backpack.
  • Wortox wearing a Backpack.
  • Wurt wearing a Backpack.
  • Backpack as seen in the poster for the The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You update.

“You could fit a whole lot of science in there.”
–Wilson
“I could fit like a million lighters in here.”
–Willow
“Is for carry more thing.”
–Wolfgang
“A pack. Now I can carry an even heavier burden.”
–Wendy
“UPGRADED STORAGE MODULE”
–WX-78
“I could fit many books in here.”
–Wickerbottom
“That's my rucksack.”
–Woodie
“I wish there were porters around here.”
–Maxwell
“A fancy free way to bear my impedimenta.”
–Wagstaff
“A pörtable armöry.”
–Wigfrid
“It's like a second abdomen we can store things in.”
–Webber
“To haul my stuff around.”
–Walani
“It has my back.”
–Warly
“I be packin' now.”
–Woodlegs
“A SACK FOR YONDER WAND'RING”
–Wilba
“For more things carrying”
–Wormwood
“Finally! My pockets were getting full.”
–Wheeler
“I don't mind playing pack mule.”
–Winona
“Behold this burden on my back!”
–Wortox
“For carry all the things!”
–Wurt
“Perfect for carrying supplies! And bugs!”
–Walter
“I could always use the extra pocket space.”
–Wanda

The '''Backpack''' is a [[Survival]] [[Item]] that serves to expand the [[character]]'s inventory. It requires 4 [[Cut Grass]] and 4 [[Twig]]s to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. The Backpack itself cannot be placed in the inventory or in [[Chest]]s and only one may be equipped at a time.

When a Backpack is created, it automatically equips itself in the Chest Slot if it is available. Wearing the Backpack provides 8 additional inventory slots. This item uses the same equipment slot as [[Armor]] and Vests, which means wearing one requires sacrificing the benefits of the other. Unlike other items that go into the Chest slot, Backpacks do not have durability.

Although Backpacks cannot be placed in the inventory or in Chests, they can be dropped on the ground and used for storage. Any item hovered over a Backpack while it's on the ground will display the 'Store' option, allowing the pack to be filled without equipping it.

== {{Pic|32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
In all DLCs, the Backpack is a flammable item. When set on [[fire]], everything inside won't be affected by fire but the Backpack will eventually burn to [[Ashes]], dropping anything it had inside. Care should be taken when dropping a backpack near a [[campfire]] to prevent it from burning. The backpack is not waterproof, and can thus get soaked and drain the player's [[Sanity]], but items in the backpack will stay completely dry. If the player does not have full water protection, it is recommended to keep one's fuel and food items in a backpack during the rain.

In the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC, Backpacks can be bought in the [[Pigg and Pigglet's General Store]] for 5 [[Oinc]]s.

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* The Backpack was added in the ''[[Version History#November 27.2C 2012.C2.A0.E2.80.93 The Birds and the Bees .26 Dogs That Want to Kill You|The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You]]'' update.

=={{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* A backpack can be burned and extinguished to be able to keep it in one's inventory. Relogging will reset this.
* Sometimes the player can't pick up a backpack in the Caves if it is dropped due to the player's death.
